summary: Complete x402 settlement stack: settlement.ts provides pure validation logic, x402-settle edge function runs server-side with service-role key, settlement-client.ts provides browser-safe invocation via supabase.functions.invoke. Validates all 6 economics dimensions (existence, idempotency, amount, chain, recipient, proof presence). Uses optimistic locking for race condition safety. 23 tests cover all assertion paths.

codeQuality.structure: Clean three-layer architecture: (1) Pure validation logic in settlement.ts, (2) Server-side edge function in supabase/functions/x402-settle/index.ts, (3) Browser-safe client in settlement-client.ts. Well-documented with clear JSDoc and usage comments.
codeQuality.security: Secrets (service-role key) stay server-side in the edge function. Browser code uses supabase.functions.invoke which handles auth automatically. Settlement proof validated against all invoice economics before marking paid. Optimistic locking prevents race conditions.
codeQuality.testCoverage: 23 tests covering: invoice creation shape (VAL-PAY-001), successful settlement with proof recording (VAL-PAY-002), all invalid proof rejection paths (VAL-PAY-003), server-side edge function invocation (VAL-PAY-004), economics matching and double-payment idempotency (VAL-PAY-005).
codeQuality.patterns: Follows existing edge function patterns (CORS headers, Deno.serve). Uses existing Supabase client. Case-insensitive wallet comparison is a good practice.
